So, now that we understand all the terminology, here's how we actually optimize our pages rather than our sales process, which is primarily what I talk to most people about, but this is actually how you optimize the tech side of it. So there's four components to this. There's page load optimization, the tools that we're going to use, split testing done properly and the tools to do that, and then event fire picture so you can track this properly. So those are kind of the four pieces to this. So first one we're going to cover is page load optimization. And so this is kind of your baseline and this is what you're going to always go back to to test against. I use webpageses.org, there's a million of them. You can use whichever one you want, this one's free, and so that's what I'm going to do. So what I want to optimize or show you is I'm going to show you two funnels. The first one is my book funnel. So let's start this test here. Of course, I'm going to have to optimize everything that has a car in it, joys of being alive, isn't this great? I think there's, I don't know if there's cars in that one. So while we're waiting for this test to happen, what I'm going to show you is this is the actual page. So you can see that I have a lot of stuff. I've got all these images on this page, lots and lots and lots of stuff, more images, I got a video, I've got just more images, tons and tons of stuff. This is a long, long page. And so if we're going to look here, what I want to also show you while we're testing this one out is webpagetest.org, we'll go to number two, and let's do the, so you can see I obviously check a lot of these on here. So this is a different page. So what you can see here, as soon as there's about to be a third test that completes, once you have all three, it will give you the breakdown of everything that has to do with this page. Now I've optimized the shit out of this page. And so you can see here, like my actual, my total load time is 18 seconds, which sounds horrible. But the first bits here are, you know, five seconds, which is okay. I like to try and keep it under four. This is obviously a huge page, a big sales page. Now the next one I want to show you is a typical pure opt-in funnel, which is like this. Okay, so you see how quickly this one loaded, right? Super, super, super easy, very, very fast to load here. Right? Now let's take a look at how that one did. Okay? You can look at A's all across the board. Total time to load was 2.7 seconds. And you know, it's, this one is ridiculously fast, right? And that's why the test didn't even take that long. Now what I want to, the reason I'm bringing this up is that the number one thing that most people don't understand about page loading optimization is that almost everything comes down to speed. And so most people lose like half their traffic or more because their page doesn't load fast enough. It took me, so when I started the optimization of this one, this number here was at, I think like 17 seconds, which was insane, right? And my total load time, I think was over a minute, which is crazy, right? But if I didn't know any better, I would just look at someone else's funnel and funnel hack them, right? And then lo and behold, like why isn't my funnel converting? It's because the load time is so slow. And so this is what we're going to constantly be going back to, to reference, to see if we've improved things, okay? And so here's, here's the other one here, so you can see speed, compression of image, you see this is A's. And so I've done all of this stuff ahead of time to make sure that we are good to go. And so I'm going to walk you through each of the tools that I use to compress things, to speed things up, and ultimately make pages convert faster.
